clinical_coherence.notes: Excellent clinical picture of a 28F with optic neuritis and prior sensory relapse. Exam findings (RAPD, Lhermitte, Babinski, hyperreflexia) are internally consistent. One issue: sodium is 153 mEq/L (hypernatremia) which is unexplained, clinically implausible in a healthy 28-year-old presenting with optic neuritis, and not addressed anywhere in the case. The lab interpretation section even states BMP is 'within normal limits' despite flagging Na 153 as abnormal.

tool_output_consistency.notes: MRI findings perfectly match MS with Dawson fingers, periventricular/juxtacortical/infratentorial/spinal cord lesions. CSF shows oligoclonal bands and elevated IgG index. VEP shows prolonged P100 latency in the affected eye. OCT shows RNFL thinning. All concordant with RRMS diagnosis. AQP4 and MOG negative appropriately.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G35 correct. McDonald 2017 criteria properly applied. Differential is well-calibrated with appropriate likelihoods. Critical actions (MRI, LP, NMO/MOG antibodies, IV methylprednisolone, DMT) are all clinically sound. Contraindicated actions are accurate (no chronic oral steroids, no brain biopsy, no delay in DMT).

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic RRMS presentation with textbook findings: young woman, optic neuritis, prior sensory relapse, Dawson fingers on MRI, positive OCBs. This is the prototypical straightforward MS case.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 28-year-old Northern European female is the archetypal MS demographic. BMI 22.8, nurse occupation, family history of autoimmune disease (Hashimoto, RA) all realistic.

educational_value.notes: Excellent teaching case covering McDonald criteria, optic neuritis evaluation, CSF analysis, VEP/OCT utility, and DMT initiation. The prior sensory episode attributed to cervical radiculopathy is a realistic misdiagnosis pattern.

issues_found: Sodium 153 mEq/L (hypernatremia) is clinically implausible and unexplained in this patient; lab interpretation incorrectly states BMP is within normal limits
Minor formatting issue: VEP abnormal_values_summary has redundant unit strings (e.g., '128 ms ms')
strengths: Textbook RRMS presentation with perfectly concordant multimodal data
Rich follow-up outputs including VEP, OCT, spine MRI, repeat MRI, and drug interaction check
Ground truth reasoning points are comprehensive and clinically accurate
Prior misdiagnosis of sensory relapse as cervical radiculopathy is a realistic clinical vignette
